name: 08 - Realistic User & Workload Analytics
description: Analyst-style questions about people: top creators, top assignees, top resolvers, workload distribution, unassigned queue, response/resolution rates per user, role mapping. Every question has a clear single answer + ExpectedSql. Designed for the same Tickets/AspNetUsers/AspNetRoles graph the production app exposes.

Scenarios.Question: Show the top 10 users by number of tickets created.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 10 u.UserName, COUNT(*) AS Cnt FROM AspNetUsers u JOIN Tickets t ON t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.UserName ORDER BY Cnt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show the top 10 assignees by number of tickets assigned to them.
Scenarios.Category: TOP N + GROUP BY + COUNT on different FK
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 10 u.UserName, COUNT(*) AS Cnt FROM AspNetUsers u JOIN Tickets t ON t.AssignedToUserId = u.Id WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.UserName ORDER BY Cnt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show the top 10 users by number of tickets they have resolved.
Scenarios.Category: TOP N on ResolvedByUserId with IS NOT NULL
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 10 u.UserName, COUNT(*) AS Cnt FROM AspNetUsers u JOIN Tickets t ON t.ResolvedByUserId = u.Id WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.UserName ORDER BY Cnt DESC

Scenarios.Question: How many tickets are unassigned?
Scenarios.Category: COUNT with IS NULL on FK
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Total FROM Tickets WHERE AssignedToUserId IS NULL AND IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: List unassigned tickets.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TicketNumber, Title FROM Tickets WHERE AssignedToUserId IS NULL AND IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: List users who have not created any tickets.
Scenarios.Category: Anti-join — users with no tickets created
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.UserName, u.Email FROM AspNetUsers u WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0)

Scenarios.Question: What is the average number of tickets per creator?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT AVG(CAST(Cnt AS DECIMAL(10,2))) AS AvgTicketsPerUser FROM (SELECT CreatedByUserId, COUNT(*) AS Cnt FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY CreatedByUserId) x

Scenarios.Question: Show users with their roles.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.UserName, r.Name AS Role FROM AspNetUsers u JOIN AspNetUserRoles ur ON ur.UserId = u.Id JOIN AspNetRoles r ON r.Id = ur.RoleId

Scenarios.Question: How many users are in each role?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT r.Name AS Role, COUNT(*) AS UserCount FROM AspNetUserRoles ur JOIN AspNetRoles r ON r.Id = ur.RoleId GROUP BY r.Name ORDER BY UserCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: Which users have created more than 5 tickets?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.UserName, COUNT(*) AS Cnt FROM AspNetUsers u JOIN Tickets t ON t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.UserName HAVING COUNT(*) > 5 ORDER BY Cnt DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show tickets with their assignee username and email.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, u.UserName, u.Email FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.AssignedToUserId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show average resolution time in hours per resolver.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.UserName, AVG(CAST(DATEDIFF(hour, t.CreatedAt, t.ResolvedAt) AS DECIMAL(10,2))) AS AvgHours FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.ResolvedByUserId WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.UserName ORDER BY AvgHours ASC

Scenarios.Question: How many open tickets does each assignee currently have?
Scenarios.Category: GROUP BY assignee + filter on status
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.UserName, COUNT(*) AS OpenCount FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.AssignedToUserId JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.IsClosedState = 0 AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.UserName ORDER BY OpenCount DESC

Scenarios.Question: List inactive users.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT UserName, Email FROM AspNetUsers WHERE IsActive = 0

Scenarios.Question: How many active users are there?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Total FROM AspNetUsers WHERE IsActive = 1

Scenarios.Question: For each user, show how many tickets they created and how many they resolved.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.UserName, SUM(CASE WHEN t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Created, SUM(CASE WHEN t.ResolvedByUserId = u.Id AND t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Resolved FROM AspNetUsers u LEFT JOIN Tickets t ON (t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id OR t.ResolvedByUserId = u.Id) AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.UserName ORDER BY Created DESC

Scenarios.Question: How many users belong to each entity?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT e.Name AS Entity, COUNT(*) AS UserCount FROM AspNetUsers u JOIN Entitys e ON e.Id = u.EntityId GROUP BY e.Name ORDER BY UserCount DESC
